questions.question: What is the difference between a rational agent and an omniscient agent?
expectedPoints: Rational agents make decisions based on available information to maximize expected performance
Omniscient agents have complete knowledge and always choose the objectively best action
Rationality is achievable in practice while omniscience is a theoretical concept

questions.question: Explain the concept of bounded rationality and its significance in artificial intelligence.
expectedPoints: Definition: Making rational decisions within computational constraints
Introduced by Herbert Simon to address practical limitations
Agents seek satisfactory rather than optimal solutions
Important for real-world applications with resource constraints
Relates to heuristic methods and approximate reasoning
Fundamental for designing practical AI systems
